festivals.name: Holi
festivals.description: The Festival of Colours transforms India with exuberant powder fights, bonfires, and street celebrations.

festivals.name: Diwali
festivals.description: The Festival of Lights illuminates all of India with oil lamps, fireworks, and the triumph of light over darkness.

festivals.name: Pushkar Camel Fair
festivals.description: The world's largest camel fair draws 50,000 camels and half a million visitors to this sacred desert town.

destinations.name: Delhi
destinations.description: India's historic capital layers Mughal grandeur — Red Fort, Humayun's Tomb — over a buzzing modern megacity.

destinations.name: Agra (Taj Mahal)
destinations.description: The Taj Mahal, built by Emperor Shah Jahan as a monument to love, is one of the world's most recognised buildings.

destinations.name: Jaipur
destinations.description: The Pink City of Rajasthan dazzles with the Amber Fort, Hawa Mahal, and a centuries-old bazaar.

destinations.name: Varanasi
destinations.description: Hinduism's holiest city on the Ganges has been a centre of spirituality for 3,000 years, famous for its burning ghats.

destinations.name: Rishikesh
destinations.description: The yoga capital of the world perches on the Ganges foothills where the Beatles once meditated with the Maharishi.

destinations.name: Pushkar
destinations.description: One of India's five sacred dhams, this tiny lakeside desert town hosts the world's largest camel fair each November.

destinations.name: Jodhpur
destinations.description: The Blue City is dominated by the mighty Mehrangarh Fort, one of India's largest, looking down over indigo-painted houses.

destinations.name: Udaipur
destinations.description: The City of Lakes dazzles with marble palaces shimmering on Lake Pichola, often called Venice of the East.
